summary: Analyzed a minimal techno, atmospheric techno project with 0 tracks, 0 clips, at 120 BPM. Found 5 potential improvements. Generated 9 completion actions (1 critical, 4 high priority).

actions.title: Add or Identify Kick Track
actions.description: No kick drum track was confidently detected. Add a kick MIDI/audio track with a 4-on-the-floor pattern. Consider layering a sub punch (50-80Hz) with a click transient.
actions.rationale: A kick is the rhythmic foundation of all techno music.

actions.title: Add Outro / Exit Section
actions.description: No outro section detected. Add an 8-16 bar outro that gradually strips back elements, leaving only kick and a fading element to allow DJ mixing out.
actions.rationale: Tracks without outros are difficult to mix out gracefully in a live DJ context.

actions.title: Add Breakdown Section
actions.description: Insert a 16-32 bar breakdown that removes the kick and bass, keeping only atmospheric elements. This creates contrast and anticipation before the drop.
actions.rationale: Breakdowns are essential for energy management in a DJ set context.

actions.title: Add Bass / Sub Element
actions.description: No bass track detected. Add a sub bass (30-80Hz) that locks to the kick pattern or runs a continuous groove. Consider a single-note bass with filter automation for movement.
actions.rationale: Sub bass provides low-end weight and energy on large sound systems.

actions.title: Build Outro Strip-Back Sequence
actions.description: Create a 16-bar outro where elements drop out gradually: first remove leads and synths (bar 1-4), then percussion (bar 5-8), leaving kick+bass to bar 12, then only kick to bar 16. Fade kick with volume automation.
actions.rationale: A proper outro is essential for live DJ mixing — allows smooth transition to the next track.

actions.title: Add Hi-Hat Layer
actions.description: No hi-hat track detected. Add a closed hi-hat on 8th or 16th notes and layer with an open hat on off-beats. Consider a ride for the groove section.
actions.rationale: Hi-hats provide rhythmic density and forward momentum.

actions.title: Strengthen Percussion Layer
actions.description: Add 1-2 additional percussion tracks (shakers, congas, industrial percussion, or noise bursts) to create rhythmic complexity. Layer at 1/16th or 1/32nd note offsets from the main groove.
actions.rationale: Techno benefits from layered percussion for hypnotic groove depth.

actions.title: Add Resonance Automation
actions.description: Automate filter resonance on basslines or synth tracks through peak sections. A rising resonance (Q) from 0.5 to 2.0 creates tension and urgency.
actions.rationale: Resonance automation adds harmonic tension absent from purely gain-based dynamics.

actions.title: Add Atmospheric Drone/Texture
actions.description: Add a long, sustained drone or textural layer that runs through breakdown and intro sections. A slowly evolving pad or resampled noise with high reverb works well for dark techno atmospheres.
actions.rationale: Textural layers provide depth and atmosphere, especially in breakdowns.

weaknesses: No kick drum track detected — low-end foundation absent or unnamed
No bass/sub track detected — low-end continuity may be weak
No outro section detected — arrangement may end abruptly at bar 0
Last 8 bars appear to have no active tracks — ending may be missing or unfinished
No return tracks detected — reverb and delay sends may be absent
